entry.title: Impressions of How the Steel was Tempered

article.p: Reading How the Steel was Tempered, I am struck by the way the narrative takes a definite position—it is for us, children of the future!—without cloaking itself in the viewpoints of depicted characters or that of an active narrator. How unsophisticated and innocent it must seem to those acquainted with the “neutrality” of bourgeois fiction, which deviates from the viewpoint of the bourgeoisie only to relate the views of a character—who naturally lives in a different world.

I wish that Things.app made it possible for me to mark that a task is in progress. There is something about the ability to do this that fits well with my own need to lower the mental and spiritual weight of my pile of tasks.
